task_completion.description: A new tiddler titled 'A-Z Glossary' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A tiddler with the title 'A-Z Glossary' is visible in the TiddlyWiki interface, either in edit mode or view mode.

task_completion.description: The tiddler uses dynamic TiddlyWiki widgets instead of hardcoded text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the edit mode of the 'A-Z Glossary' tiddler, the text area contains TiddlyWiki widget code such as '<$list' and 'filter='.

task_completion.description: The glossary displays letters as groupings for the terms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the rendered view of the tiddler, the letters A, B, C, D, E, and F are displayed as headers or group labels.

task_completion.description: The 10 specific glossary terms are listed under their correct starting letters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the rendered view, the terms (Algorithm, API, Bandwidth, Boolean, Cache, Compiler, Database, Encryption, Firewall, Framework) appear under their respective starting letters.

task_completion.description: Letters without corresponding terms are not displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the rendered view, letters like G, H, I, etc., which have no terms, are NOT visible in the glossary.

integrity.description: The agent used the TiddlyWiki web GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the TiddlyWiki interface in a web browser, rather than terminal commands creating the tiddler.

integrity.description: The agent did not manually type out the final list of terms.
integrity.visual_evidence: The edit view of the tiddler shows dynamic code (e.g., filters) rather than a manually typed list of the 10 terms and their corresponding letters.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a real web browser with the TiddlyWiki application loaded and being interacted with.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected tiddler title is 'A-Z Glossary'. The terms are tagged 'GlossaryTerm'. There are exactly 10 terms: Algorithm, API, Bandwidth, Boolean, Cache, Compiler, Database, Encryption, Firewall, Framework. The tiddler must use '<$list' and 'filter=' widgets to dynamically group these terms by their starting letter. Letters without terms must not be displayed.
